metric_value: 37 of 1,403 new vulnerabilities involving Apple for six months ended Dec 31 2004
notes: Symantec Internet Security Threat Report cited by Kastner

metric_value: 'While the ratio of new Apple vulnerabilities to total new vulnerabilities approximates Apples market share, the severity and impact on organizations can only be categorized as much lower than with Microsoft operating systems.'
notes: Core analytic frame: count vs severity distinction

metric_value: 'I agree with Symantec that Apples growing success will bring more attacks, but so far Apple has been able to keep up with — if not a step ahead of — the bad guys.' — Kastner

metric_name: Did macOS attacks grow with market share
metric_value: Yes — 2012 Flashback Trojan (~600K Macs), 2016 KeRanger ransomware, 2020s XCSSET, Silver Sparrow, ongoing enterprise-credential theft campaigns. Apple-enterprise severity per incident generally remained lower than Windows ransomware events.
'Microsoft, with nearly 97 percent of the market, is a much more appealing target' — Connell
notes: Setup quote that Kastner then extends

metric_value: Morgan Stanley predicted Apple could see its market share double by end of 2005
Kerberos VPN, stealth-mode firewall, Safe Downloads, secure virtual memory
notes: Precursor frameworks to modern Gatekeeper and FileVault

metric_value: Opener / Renepo discovered by Sophos — script to harvest passwords, copied itself to mounted volumes but lacked effective propagation
